name: Cleartronic, Inc.
summary: Cleartronic, Inc. (CLRI) operates through its wholly owned subsidiary ReadyOp Communications, Inc., offering web-based planning and communications platforms including ReadyOp, ReadyMed, and Alastar, primarily as SaaS on annual contracts. The company also manufactures and sells AudioMate 360 IP Gateways for unified group communications. Clients include federal, state, and local government agencies, hospitals, school districts, utilities, and corporations. ReadyOp serves as the continuity of operations (COOP) platform for multiple federal agencies.

products.name: ReadyOp
products.description: Web-based SaaS planning and communications platform for operations management and emergency response, offered on annual or multi-year contracts.

products.name: ReadyMed
products.description: Web-based secure communications platform for healthcare industry, providing patient tracking, monitoring, and secure recordkeeping.

products.name: Alastar
products.description: Acquired software platform being supported for existing clients and transitioning functionality into the ReadyOp platform.

products.name: AudioMate 360 IP Gateway
products.description: Proprietary Internet Protocol Gateway for unified group communications, sold directly and through Value Added Resellers.

partnerships.partner_name: University of South Florida Research Foundation, Inc.
partnerships.description: Exclusive licensing agreement for certain patent rights effective April 25, 2017, with 3% royalty on licensed products and processes.
